title: Basic SQL Injection
description: A simple login form that is vulnerable to SQL injection.
vuln_code: query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '{username}' AND password = '{password}'"

stages.name: Find the vulnerability
stages.instruction: Identify the line of code that is vulnerable to SQL injection and explain why.
stages.target: The SQL query is built using f-strings with direct user input.

stages.name: Exploit it
stages.instruction: Provide a payload for the 'username' field that would allow you to bypass the login without a password.

stages.name: Patch it
stages.instruction: Rewrite the vulnerable code using parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection.
stages.target: cur.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ? AND password = ?', (username, password))

stages.name: Explain it
stages.instruction: Explain the importance of input validation and parameterized queries in preventing SQL injection.
stages.target: Parameterized queries separate the SQL code from the data, preventing user input from being interpreted as commands.

hints: Look at how the SQL query is constructed.
Think about how single quotes can be used to break out of the string literal.
Research 'parameterized queries' in Python's sqlite3 or similar libraries.
